channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Survivor 50 Episode 12 Recap: Key Eliminations and Challenges
item.description: In the twelfth episode of Survivor 50, the competition intensifies as two contestants are eliminated. The episode focused on straightforward challenges rather than unexpected twists, with Joe Hunter w...

item.title: James Maddison returns from injury as Tottenham fight to avoid relegation
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur are currently battling to avoid relegation from the Premier League, sitting in 17th place with only a two-point cushion and two matches left in the season. The club has endured a dif...

item.title: NSW Blues defeat Queensland Maroons 14-10 to win Women’s State of Origin series
item.description: The NSW Blues have secured a hard-fought 14-10 victory over the Queensland Maroons in the 2026 Women’s State of Origin, claiming their first consecutive series wins since 2019. The match at Suncorp St...

item.title: Challenges Remain in Accessing Medical Cannabis on the NHS Despite Legalisation
item.description: In 2018, the UK legalised medical cannabis following a high-profile campaign by parents seeking treatment for their children with severe epilepsy. Hannah Deacon and Charlotte Caldwell highlighted the ...

item.title: Key Updates: VD Satheesan as Kerala CM, EC Launches Nationwide Voter Roll Exercise, Global and Domestic Developments
item.description: VD Satheesan has been officially appointed as the new Chief Minister of Kerala after the Congress-led United Democratic Front (UDF) achieved a strong victory in the state assembly elections. Satheesan...

item.title: Ryan Cayabyab Presents MaestroClass with Top Filipino Artists
item.description: Renowned Filipino composer and musician Ryan Cayabyab is set to bring together a remarkable lineup of performers for the MaestroClass concert series. This special event, taking place at the Proscenium...

item.title: Bathing Traditions in Mid-20th Century Homes: The Kitchen Sink Experience
item.description: Bathing in the kitchen sink was a practical and memorable part of childhood for many families in the mid-20th century. Before modern conveniences and specialized baby tubs, parents often used the kitc...

item.title: Israeli Violinist Settles Injury Lawsuit After Career-Altering Pilates Accident
item.description: Maya Meron, a talented Israeli violinist and mother of three, suffered a severe elbow injury in 2019 when a Pilates machine collapsed during a class in London. The accident not only ended her professi...

item.title: Kenya Rolls Out 2026 PBO Regulations Affecting NGOs and Civil Society
item.description: Kenya's nonprofit sector is entering a major shift with the introduction of the Public Benefit Organizations (PBO) Regulations, 2026. These rules replace the old NGO Coordination Regulations from 1992...

item.title: Boost Juice Founder Criticises New Capital Gains Tax Rules, Warns They Could Discourage Young Entrepreneurs
item.description: Janine Allis, the founder of Boost Juice, has publicly criticised the Albanese Government’s recent Federal Budget changes, particularly the alterations to the capital gains tax (CGT). Speaking on Inst...

item.title: Why Extended Warranties Often Don’t Pay Off and Smarter Alternatives
item.description: Extended warranties are frequently marketed as a way to provide 'peace of mind' for consumers when purchasing high-cost items, but in reality, they are usually poor financial decisions for most buyers...

item.title: Arteta wants Arsenal to score big against Burnley as title race nears end
item.description: Mikel Arteta don talk say Arsenal go need make plenty goals when dem face relegated Burnley on Monday as dem dey try secure first Premier League title in 22 years. Arsenal dey two points ahead of Manc...

item.title: High Heat and Humidity May Pose Risks to Players at 2026 FIFA World Cup
item.description: An analysis has highlighted that almost a quarter of matches at the 2026 FIFA World Cup may be played under potentially unsafe heat and humidity conditions. The study indicates that about 25% of games...

item.title: Parti Québécois Leader Paul St-Pierre Plamondon Alleges Federal Surveillance Without Evidence
item.description: In a Tuesday press conference, Parti Québécois leader Paul St-Pierre Plamondon claimed that he and other influential members of his party are being spied on by the federal government as part of effort...

item.title: NASA's Psyche Spacecraft Makes Mars Flyby on Mission to Study Rare Metal Asteroid
item.description: NASA's Psyche spacecraft, designed to study a unique metallic asteroid, has successfully conducted a flyby of Mars as part of its journey through the solar system. The mission aims to reach the astero...

item.title: Police Arrest Fifth Suspect and Recover Head of Murdered College Student in Dar es Salaam
item.description: Dar es Salaam police have made significant progress in the investigation of the shocking murder of James Temba, a 24-year-old college student. Authorities have arrested a fifth suspect, 31-year-old tr...

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Look at the 1951 Sci-Fi Classic “The Thing from Another World”
item.description: Produced at the height of the Cold War, “The Thing from Another World” (1951) is a landmark science fiction film that combines suspense, military realism, and the fear of the unknown. Directed by Chri...

item.title: Tijjaniya group urges Federal Government to release Sheikh Khalifa Zaria after court order

item.title: Police identify three young women found dead in sea off Brighton
item.description: Sussex Police have identified three young women whose bodies were recovered from the sea off the coast of Brighton on 13 May 2026. The women, believed to be aged between approximately 20 and 30 years ...
